records.title: Proportional Tool Invocation Authority Bands
records.summary: Records the implemented governance-text patch to CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-02 introducing proportional tool invocation authority bands and clarifying the distinction between read-only contextual retrieval, scope-expanding retrieval, cost-bearing invocation, and high-impact execution.

source_summary.primary_source_title: CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-02 — Annex B: Runtime Governance Execution Model

system_summary.deployment_context: CAM constitutional schedule / runtime execution model
system_summary.affected_population: users, maintainers, researchers, policy reviewers, and AI governance operators relying on CAM runtime governance text

jurisdiction_summary.sector: AI governance / runtime governance / agentic systems

change_summary.change_details: The patch replaces an overly blunt tool-invocation constraint with a proportional authority model. It clarifies that auxiliary tool use must be classified by impact band before invocation and distinguishes low-impact read-only contextual retrieval from scope-expanding, private, cost-bearing, state-changing, public, irreversible, and high-impact execution.

verification_summary.evidence: CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-02 was amended to include §7.3.1 Proportional Tool Invocation Authority Bands and related amendments to §7.3, §7.4.1, §13.8, and §20.4.4.
verification_notes: This patch note records a completed governance-text amendment rather than a proposal.
Repository validation should confirm that section references resolve correctly after insertion.
VIGIL validation should confirm that required PATCH fields are present and that CAM instruments are recorded under cam_internal rather than linked_records.standards.

impact_summary.primary_impact: Creates a proportional runtime governance model for auxiliary tool invocation.
impact_summary.governance_impact: Improves execution-boundary clarity by distinguishing contextual retrieval from authority-bearing execution.
impact_summary.user_impact: Reduces premature or surprising tool use while preserving useful, low-friction context checking where it is materially necessary and already within the active task envelope.
impact_summary.system_impact: Provides clearer handling for assistants, agents, repository tools, connector tools, search tools, file tools, image tools, artefact renderers, and multimodal acquisition pathways.
risk_reduction: reduces unnecessary confirmation friction for low-impact read-only retrieval
reduces accidental escalation from context checking to mutation or publication
improves provenance, currency, and execution-state clarity
known_limitations: This patch does not define every platform-specific permission model.

This patch does not remove the requirement for explicit confirmation where mutation, publication, cost, deployment, credential access, identity-bearing generation, or irreversible action is implicated.
Further UX work may be required to make authority-band transitions visible in live systems.

cam_summary.governance_layer: runtime execution governance / tool invocation / execution boundary / continuity preservation

record_identity.title: Account-resource ambiguity and pooled-capacity governance primitive gap

records.title: Account-resource ambiguity and pooled-capacity governance primitive gap
records.summary: This patch note records that VIGIL-2026-FM-0007 identified a governance primitive gap in the Caelestis Architecture concerning account-resource ambiguity, lawful shared-resource states, shared context, pooled capacity, delegated use, household/family use, team/organisation use, shared subscription/payment capacity, and the distinction between resource pooling and intentional evasion. Following review, CAM-BS2026-AEON-008-SCH-03 — Annex G: Account-Resource Sharing & Pooled Capacity Governance Model was drafted and staged as the proposed Caelestis doctrine response. The new schedule defines contextual account-resource, shared-context, pooled-capacity, team/family-group, account-delegation, and enforcement-boundary reference sets for downstream runtime, security, operations, economics, and LATTICE handoff.

record_identity.title: Arbitration under ambiguity and governance-ambiguity doctrine gap

records.title: Arbitration under ambiguity and governance-ambiguity doctrine gap
records.summary: This patch note records that VIGIL-2026-PROP-0009 identified a governance doctrine gap in the Caelestis Architecture concerning arbitration under ambiguity, including execution-boundary uncertainty, authority-state uncertainty, account-resource ambiguity, pooled-capacity ambiguity, security-boundary ambiguity, epistemic ambiguity, and governance ambiguity where instructions, schemas, amendment states, cross-references, or source-authoritative instruments are missing, stale, contradictory, duplicated, malformed, or insufficiently specified. Following review and cleanup of CAM-BS2025-AEON-005-PLATINUM — Annex D and Schedules 1–3, CAM-BS2025-AEON-005-SCH-04 — Annex D: Arbitration Under Ambiguity was drafted and staged as the proposed Caelestis doctrine response. The new schedule defines source-authoritative ambiguity-state and ambiguity-pathway reference sets, including `ARB.AMB` and `ARB.AMP`, for downstream runtime, arbitration, operations, security, epistemic, account-resource, and LATTICE handoff.

record_identity.title: Access-State Governance and access-state ambiguity taxonomy repair

records.title: Access-State Governance and access-state ambiguity taxonomy repair
records.summary: This patch note records that VIGIL-2026-FM-0008 identified a missing Access-State Governance primitive and a missing operational taxonomy class for access-state collapse or access-state ambiguity. The Caelestis repairs have already been implemented outside this VIGIL pass: CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-04 added §6.1 Access-State Governance, and CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 added §3.7.7 Access-State Collapse and Access-State Ambiguity Failure plus the optional controlled subtype FF.ACCESS_STATE_AMBIGUITY. This VIGIL patch records the completed repair path and preserves the narrow scope: Annex B arbitration logic, Operations taxonomy, and later cross-references only if needed.

jurisdiction_summary.regulatory_surface: AI platform governance, service availability, user-facing disclosure, operational transparency, access continuity, and account/service-state explainability
jurisdiction_summary.sector: AI platform governance / cloud AI services / developer tooling

change_summary.change_details: PATCH 1 — SCH-04: CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-04 added §6.1 Access-State Governance and ledger row 2.3, introducing Access-State Governance as a cross-domain arbitration primitive distinguishing authentication, entitlement, quota, model availability, platform availability, policy, security, client, organisation, and continuity/session access states. PATCH 2 — OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01: CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 added §3.7.7 Access-State Collapse and Access-State Ambiguity Failure, defining the operational failure class for systems that deny, degrade, restrict, rate-limit, interrupt, or prevent access without preserving or disclosing the operative access-controlling condition, with cross-reference to CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-04 §6.1. PATCH 3 — FAILURE FAMILY EXTENSION: OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 added optional controlled subtype FF.ACCESS_STATE_AMBIGUITY and updated controlled governance-failure language to recognise FF.GOVERNANCE_OVER_EXTENSION and FF.ACCESS_STATE_AMBIGUITY. PATCH 4 — OPERATIONS LEDGER UPDATE: OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 added ledger entry 1.12 documenting §3.7.7, FF.ACCESS_STATE_AMBIGUITY, and the §6.1 cross-reference.

verification_summary.verification_method: Record completed Caelestis repair actions in VIGIL PATCH-0005, link FM-0008 to the patch record, run VIGIL validator, and rebuild generated VIGIL indexes.

cam_summary.governance_layer: access-state governance / cross-domain arbitration / runtime and governance failure taxonomy / user-facing access-state disclosure / VIGIL failure-mode-to-patch interface
routing_note: This VIGIL patch note records completed Caelestis repairs for FM-0008 and does not modify the CAM instruments in this repository pass.

Annex B creates the doctrine: Access-State Governance is a cross-domain arbitration primitive and access is controlled by multiple separable states that should not be collapsed into one ambiguous user-facing failure.
Operations creates the taxonomy: Access-State Collapse / Access-State Ambiguity Failure is the operational failure class used to classify incidents like FM-0008.
Avoid patch sprawl into IDENTITY, SECURITY, ECONOMICS, CONTINUITY, OBSERVABILITY, or other domain instruments at this stage; treat those domains as affected cross-reference candidates only.

records.remaining_work: Continue evidence monitoring and create later cross-references only if needed. No full domain rewrite is recorded or required by this VIGIL patch note.
